intro: Achras breviloba, commonly known as the Mayan plum, is a plant species in the Sapotaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central America and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Achras breviloba include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
